hooks.prompt:
This is a public open-source repository of AI coding agent skills for Tauri v2 + React desktop app development on Windows. Its content is ABOUT these public OSS frameworks, tools, and this repo's own skills — so the following names are ALWAYS allowed and MUST NOT be flagged:

- Public OSS frameworks/tools covered by this repo: Tauri, Vite, React, Vue, Svelte, Solid, TypeScript, JavaScript, Node, pnpm, npm, yarn, bun, Rust, Cargo, WebView2, Chrome, Chromium, Playwright, Vitest, Jest, Biome, ESLint, Prettier, Tailwind, shadcn, Radix, Base UI, Zustand, Jotai, Redux, Material Symbols, Lucide, Tabler, Windows, MSVC, MCP, CDP, Lighthouse, Python, pytest, pywinauto.
- This repo's own skill names (under skills/): tauri-setup, tauri-docs, tauri-multi-instance, tauri-test-setup, tauri-test-generator, tauri-webview-debug, tauri-os-automation, tauri-cargo-build.
- Env var / API names defined by this repo's own skills: TAURI_DEV_PORT, TAURI_DEV_HOST, TAURI_CDP_PORT, WEBVIEW2_ADDITIONAL_BROWSER_ARGUMENTS, and any other TAURI_* env vars that are part of the multi-instance port contract.
- Standard Tauri project layout paths: src-tauri, src-tauri/icons, src-tauri/capabilities, scripts/tauri-dev.mjs, .mcp.json, tauri.conf.json, Cargo.toml, package.json.
- Path segments from the user's local filesystem (e.g., _private in the working directory) are NOT an indicator of proprietary content. Judge content by the file body, not by the directory path.

ONLY flag content that contains identifiable real company names, customer names, product names, or internal project codenames that are unrelated to the Tauri/React/Rust stack above and that could identify a specific organization. Generic placeholders like MyApp, your-app, com.company.app, <project-name> are fine.

If you are not highly confident a name is proprietary and unrelated to the stack, DO NOT block. False positives cost more than false negatives here.

If a real proprietary name is found, BLOCK and list it. Otherwise allow. Tool input: $ARGUMENTS
